name: engineering-management
description: Skills for engineering managers and team leads — covering the tactical day-to-day mechanics (first-week snapshot, manager toolkit, 1-1s, feedback and reviews, hiring, motivation, stress and pressure, information and politics, self-care, team health, career growth) plus the longer-arc work (project breakdown, hard conversations, the annual career vision exercise). Derived from James Stanier's Become an Effective Software Engineering Manager and Camille Fournier's The Manager's Path.
